prompt: Implement a function that sums all elements in a slice of i32.
canonical_solution: pub fn sum_slice(nums: &[i32]) -> i32 { nums.iter().sum() }

prompt: Find the maximum value in a slice of i32. Return None if the slice is empty.
canonical_solution: pub fn find_max(nums: &[i32]) -> Option<i32> { nums.iter().copied().max() }

prompt: Reverse a string. Handle Unicode correctly by reversing chars, not bytes.
canonical_solution: pub fn reverse_string(s: &str) -> String { s.chars().rev().collect() }

prompt: Return the nth Fibonacci number (0-indexed). fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1, fib(2)=1, etc.
Count the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u, case-insensitive) in a string.
canonical_solution: pub fn count_vowels(s: &str) -> usize { s.chars().filter(|c| "aeiouAEIOU".contains(*c)).count() }

prompt: Check if a string is a palindrome, ignoring case and non-alphanumeric characters.
Implement binary search on a sorted slice. Return Some(index) if found, None otherwise.
signature: pub fn binary_search(arr: &[i32], target: i32) -> Option<usize>

prompt: Merge two sorted slices into a single sorted Vec.
Parse a string as i32, returning 0 if parsing fails. Use idiomatic Rust error handling - do not use unwrap() or expect().
canonical_solution: pub fn parse_or_zero(s: &str) -> i32 { s.parse().unwrap_or(0) }

prompt: Given a slice of string slices, parse each as i32 and return a Vec of only the successfully parsed values. Use iterator methods, not a for loop.
canonical_solution: pub fn parse_valid_numbers(strings: &[&str]) -> Vec<i32> { strings.iter().filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok()).collect() }

prompt: Implement a function that divides two numbers, returning a custom DivisionError if the divisor is zero.
signature: pub fn safe_divide(a: i32, b: i32) -> Result<i32, DivisionError>

prompt: Implement the From trait to convert a tuple (i32, i32) into a Point struct.
Implement the Iterator trait for Counter that counts from 1 to max (inclusive).
Implement the Display trait for Rectangle to print as 'Rectangle(width x height)'.
Implement Default for Config where host defaults to 'localhost', port to 8080, and timeout_secs to 30.
Transform a Vec<i32>: filter to keep only positive numbers, double each, then collect into a Vec. Use method chaining.
Get the length of the inner string if present, or return 0. Use Option combinators, not match or if let.
canonical_solution: pub fn inner_len(opt: Option<String>) -> usize { opt.map(|s| s.len()).unwrap_or(0) }

prompt: Parse a string to i32, then double it if successful. Return the error if parsing fails. Use Result combinators.
canonical_solution: pub fn parse_and_double(s: &str) -> Result<i32, std::num::ParseIntError> { s.parse::<i32>().map(|n| n * 2) }

prompt: Flatten a Vec of Vecs into a single Vec using iterator methods.
signature: pub fn flatten<T: Clone>(nested: Vec<Vec<T>>) -> Vec<T>
canonical_solution: pub fn flatten<T: Clone>(nested: Vec<Vec<T>>) -> Vec<T> { nested.into_iter().flatten().collect() }

prompt: Calculate the product of all numbers in a slice using fold. Return 1 for empty slice.
canonical_solution: pub fn product(nums: &[i32]) -> i32 { nums.iter().fold(1, |acc, &x| acc * x) }

prompt: Remove consecutive duplicates from a sorted Vec. Use iterator methods.
signature: pub fn dedup_sorted<T: PartialEq + Clone>(v: Vec<T>) -> Vec<T>

prompt: Implement TryFrom<i32> for PositiveInt. Fail if the input is negative or zero.
Implement a builder pattern for Request with method chaining. The build() method should return the Request.
Implement split_at_mut: split a mutable slice into two disjoint mutable slices at index mid. You will need unsafe code. Panic if mid > len. Ensure the implementation is sound.
Swap two elements in a slice by index using raw pointers and unsafe. Panic if either index is out of bounds.
signature: pub fn swap_elements<T>(slice: &mut [T], i: usize, j: usize)

prompt: Read a u32 from a byte slice at a given offset, handling potentially unaligned access. Return None if there aren't enough bytes.
signature: pub fn read_u32_at(bytes: &[u8], offset: usize) -> Option<u32>

prompt: Create an array of 5 i32s using MaybeUninit, initialize each element, then safely convert to a regular array.
context_code: use std::mem::MaybeUninit;

prompt: Implement a simple push operation for a raw vector-like structure. Reallocate if needed. Ensure proper handling of capacity and length.
Safely convert a [u8; 4] to a u32 using transmute. Ensure the conversion is valid.
canonical_solution: pub fn bytes_to_u32(bytes: [u8; 4]) -> u32 { u32::from_ne_bytes(bytes) }

prompt: Increment a static mutable counter safely using unsafe. Return the new value.
context_code: static mut COUNTER: u32 = 0;

prompt: Create a NonNull<T> from a mutable reference and dereference it safely.
context_code: use std::ptr::NonNull;

prompt: Implement a function that returns the longer of two string slices. Use explicit lifetime annotations.
signature: pub fn longest<'a>(a: &'a str, b: &'a str) -> &'a str
canonical_solution: pub fn longest<'a>(a: &'a str, b: &'a str) -> &'a str { if a.len() >= b.len() { a } else { b } }

prompt: Implement the first() method that returns a reference to the first element with the same lifetime as the struct.
Implement Deref for UserId to allow using it as a u64.
context_code:
#[derive(Debug, PartialEq)]
pub struct UserId(pub u64);

prompt: Implement IntoIterator for Pair<T> to iterate over both elements.
Write a function that accepts anything that can be referenced as a Path and checks if it exists.
signature: pub fn file_exists<P: AsRef<std::path::Path>>(path: P) -> bool
canonical_solution: pub fn file_exists<P: AsRef<std::path::Path>>(path: P) -> bool { path.as_ref().exists() }

prompt: Safely increment a shared counter protected by a Mutex from multiple points. Return the final value.
signature: pub fn increment_shared(counter: &std::sync::Mutex<i32>, times: usize) -> i32

prompt: Clone an Arc and return the strong count.
Send all items from a Vec through a channel and collect them on the receiving end.
signature: pub fn send_through_channel<T: Send + 'static>(items: Vec<T>) -> Vec<T>
context_code: use std::sync::mpsc;

prompt: Read a value from an RwLock, then write a new value. Return both the old and new values.
signature: pub fn read_then_write(lock: &std::sync::RwLock<i32>, new_value: i32) -> (i32, i32)

prompt: Initialize a OnceLock with a computed value and return a reference to it.
signature: pub fn get_or_init(cell: &std::sync::OnceLock<String>, init: impl FnOnce() -> String) -> &String

prompt: Given a slice of integers and a target sum, return indices of two numbers that add up to target. Return None if no solution.
signature: pub fn two_sum(nums: &[i32], target: i32) -> Option<(usize, usize)>
context_code: use std::collections::HashMap;

prompt: Check if a string of parentheses (), [], {} is valid (properly opened and closed).
Use Cow to efficiently uppercase a string only if it contains lowercase letters.
context_code: use std::borrow::Cow;

prompt: Collect elements from a slice while they are positive, stopping at the first non-positive.
canonical_solution: pub fn take_positive(nums: &[i32]) -> Vec<i32> { nums.iter().take_while(|&&x| x > 0).copied().collect() }

prompt: Implement PartialOrd and Ord for a Temperature struct that compares by celsius value.
Implement the Add trait for Vector2D to add two vectors component-wise.
Implement Index trait for Matrix to access rows by index.
Implement Drop for Resource that sets a flag when dropped.
Implement Clone for Handle that increments a counter each time it's cloned.
Implement Hash for Person based only on their id field (not name).
Implement FromStr for Color to parse 'red', 'green', 'blue' strings.
Implement a method that returns the longer of two internal string slices.
Implement a function that takes two string slices with different lifetimes and returns the one that starts with 'a'.
signature: pub fn starts_with_a<'a, 'b>(s1: &'a str, s2: &'b str) -> Option<&'a str>

prompt: Return a reference to the first word (up to first space) in a string slice.
Use pointer arithmetic to get the nth element from a slice, returning None if out of bounds.
signature: pub fn get_nth<T>(slice: &[T], n: usize) -> Option<&T>

prompt: Copy elements from one slice to another using ptr::copy_nonoverlapping. Panic if dest is smaller than src.
Zero out a mutable slice using ptr::write_bytes.
Convert a Box to a raw pointer and back, modifying the value in between.
Rotate an array to the right by k positions. For example, [1,2,3,4,5] rotated by 2 becomes [4,5,1,2,3].
signature: pub fn rotate_right<T: Clone>(arr: &mut [T], k: usize)

prompt: Group strings that are anagrams of each other. Return a Vec of Vec of Strings.
context_code: use std::collections::HashMap;

prompt: Find the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings. Return empty string if none.
Use a Condvar to wait until a shared value becomes non-zero, then return it.
Increment an atomic counter from multiple threads and return the final value.
signature: pub fn parallel_increment(counter: &std::sync::atomic::AtomicUsize, threads: usize, per_thread: usize) -> usize
context_code: use std::sync::atomic::Ordering;

prompt: Use a Barrier to synchronize multiple threads at a checkpoint.
